steps: Mortgage per month = £1,245. Service charge per month = 1/5 × £1,245 = £249. Total monthly payment = £1,245 + £249 = £1,494. Over 18 months, total paid = £1,494 × 18 = £26,892. Wait — recalc: 1,245 ÷ 5 = 249. 1,245 + 249 = 1,494. 1,494 × 18 = (1,500 × 18) − (6 × 18) = 27,000 − 108 = 26,892. But 26,892 is option B. However, check if service charge is 1/5 of mortgage payment, so total per month = mortgage + service = mortgage + (1/5)mortgage = (6/5) × 1,245 = 1.2 × 1,245 = 1,494. Multiply by 18: 1,494 × 18 = 26,892. That matches option B. But let's verify arithmetic: 1,245 × 6/5 = 1,245 × 1.2 = 1,494. 1,494 × 10 = 14,940; ×8 = 11,952; sum = 26,892. So answer is £26,892. But option B is £26,892, option E is £26,910 — difference of £18. Possibly misreading? That would be 1/5 × (1,245 × 18) = 1/5 × 22,410 = 4,482, then total = 22,410 + 4,482 = 26,892 — same result. So B is correct. But why is E 26,910?
